description: ESM/CJS compatibility is both a type-level and runtime contract; declarations alone are insufficient.
body: Apply when package.json type, exports, require/import usage, tsconfig module target, or emitted file extension changes. TypeScript may accept an import shape while Node, Bun, bundlers, or test runners reject it at runtime. Execution action: compare source imports, emitted module format, package exports, and consumer usage. Do not rely only on skipLibCheck or ambient declarations to prove compatibility. Verification signals include package-local typecheck, runtime smoke under the intended loader, and consumer import/require tests when the package is public. Risk boundary: dual packages need explicit ESM and CJS entry expectations rather than accidental default interop. Use when TypeScript / JavaScript work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
